House Spicer of Castamere
Coat of arms 3 pepperpots on a saffron bend, upon green and silver stripes
(Paly vert and argent, three pepperpots proper on a bend tenné)
Seat Castamere
Head Lord Rolph Spicer
Region westerlands
Title Lord of Castamere
Heir Sybell Spicer
Overlord House Lannister
Founder Rolph Spicer's father
Founded 244–267 AC

House Spicer is a minor noble house of the westerlands. They blazon their arms with three black pepperpots on a saffron bend, across a field of green and silver stripes.[1][2]

Contents

  • 1 History
  • 2 Recent Events
    • 2.1 A Storm of Swords
    • 2.2 A Feast for Crows
  • 3 House Spicer at the end of the third century
  • 4 Quotes
  • 5 References

History[ ]

House Spicer is a newer house founded by a petty lord, a wealthy merchant elevated by Lord Tytos Lannister.[3] The founder's father was a trader of saffron and pepper[4] who wed a maegi from Essos.[3] This witch woman, known as Maggy the Frog to the populace of Lannisport, is alleged to have used spells[3] and love potions.[4]

House Spicer's wealth led the founder's daughter, Sybell Spicer,[5] to marry Gawen Westerling, lord of ancient but impoverished House Westerling.[4] This has made the younger generation of Westerlings poor marriage matches due to being descended of upjumped merchants.[5]

Recent Events[ ]

A Storm of Swords[ ]

After Ser Jaime Lannister escapes from Riverrun, Ser Samwell Spicer is among the men sent by Lord Tywin Lannister to discreetly search for the Kingslayer.[6]

Ser Rolph Spicer serves as castellan of the Crag, where his sister, Sybell Spicer, is lady of the castle. With Sybell's husband, Lord Gawen Westerling, a captive at Seagard,[7] Rolph yields the castle to Robb Stark, King in the North, during the storming of the Crag. Sybell discretely negotiates with House Spicer's liege lord, Tywin, in return for promises of worthy marriages for her daughters, Jeyne and Eleyna.[5] Rolph and Sybell maneuver Jeyne into treating the wounded Robb.[8] After sleeping with the girl, Robb chooses to wed her, thereby breaking his alliance with House Frey.[1] Sybell begins providing posset to Jeyne, claiming it will make her fertile.[9]

Rolph accompanies Sybell and her children when they travel with Robb to Riverrun. Catelyn Stark is distrustful of Rolph because Grey Wind behaves aggressively towards him.[1] Robb sends Rolph to deliver the hostage Martyn Lannister at the Golden Tooth.[10]

Robb is betrayed and killed by Houses Frey and Bolton at the Red Wedding.[11] Ser Kevan Lannister has King Tommen I Baratheon sign a decree raising Rolph to lordship and giving him the lands and title of Lord of Castamere.[12]

A Feast for Crows[ ]

After the siege of Riverrun, Sybell is upset when Jaime informs her that Tywin intended for her son, Ser Raynald Westerling, to marry a bastard, Joy Hill. However, according to a semi-canon source, this is a misunderstanding on Jaime's part[13] as Tywin already arranged for Joy to marry one of Lord Walder Frey's natural sons.[14] Sybell reveals that per her arrangement with Tywin, she took measures to prevent Jeyne from becoming pregnant with Robb's child.[5]

House Spicer at the end of the third century[ ]

The known Spicers during the timespan of the events described in A Song of Ice and Fire are:

  • Lord Rolph Spicer, Lord of Castamere. Formerly castellan of the Crag.
  • Lady Sybell Spicer, his sister. Wife of Lord Gawen Westerling.
  • Ser Samwell Spicer, his cousin.

Quotes[ ]

Tyrion: Could the Westerlings and Spicers be such great fools as to believe the wolf can defeat the lion?
Tywin: The greatest fools are ofttimes more clever than the men who laugh at them.[4]

—Tyrion Lannister and Tywin Lannister
